rationale: The citing paper uses Geyskens et al. (2010) as direct support for key setup assumptions, not just as a generic literature-list mention. It cites the study to justify that private labels now operate as multi-tier portfolios and to support the quality-positioning assumption that leading national brands are typically perceived as higher quality than private labels. These points feed into the citing paper's conceptual framework and hypothesis logic about which rivals are most affected by new product introductions. Although the citing paper does not reuse the cited paper's exact model, the cited work materially informs its framing.
relation_description: The citing paper treats the cited paper as prior empirical grounding for multi-tier private-label competition and relative quality positioning between national brands and private labels. This foundation is used to motivate and structure the citing paper's competitive-impact framework.
